items.title: Oil prices erase gains after Iran says U.S. talks could be pursued based on national interests
items.summary: American forces have started ninth consecutive night of strikes against Iranian targets.

items.title: Voters wary of government ownership in companies as Trump administration takes equity stakes
items.summary: About half of voters believe it isn't appropriate for the U.S. government to own stakes in companies, a new CNBC poll found.

items.title: FIFA emerges as the $9 billion winner as the biggest, brashest World Cup ever draws to a close
items.summary: The football governing body presided over the most lucrative sporting event in history and is poised to net over $9 billion in revenue for 2026.

items.title: UK's Andy Burnham becomes PM as Trump slams Britain as 'Poverty Stricken Disaster'
items.summary: The prospect of Burnham, considered more left-leaning than Starmer, replacing the incumbent prime minister sent jitters through bond markets earlier this year.

items.title: Bezos backs CuspAI as startup teams up with Nvidia to hunt for chipmaking materials
items.summary: CuspAI is part of a new wave of companies looking to use AI to solve challenges in the physical world.

items.title: Boeing CEO says company still needs 'a couple more years' to repair finances before new jet
items.summary: CEO Kelly Ortberg signalled Boeing is focused more on stabilizing its existing business than rushing to develop a successor to its best-selling 737 MAX.

items.title: Ryanair stock slides 6% as higher fuel costs amid Iran war dent profit
items.summary: Ryanair's first-quarter profit took a 34% hit as consumers delayed bookings due to the Middle East crisis, while struggling airlines face a "difficult winter."

items.title: TSMC is accelerating Arizona factory buildout to capitalize on AI 'megatrend,' CFO says
items.summary: Speaking in an interview with CNBC, TSMC's Wendell Huang said the fresh investment comes on the back of robust customer demand.

items.title: World Cup audience stayed bipartisan despite Trump's prominent role, CNBC survey finds
items.summary: Nearly half of registered voters watched the 2026 World Cup, with income and education shaping viewership more than party affiliation, the survey found.

items.title: World Cup 2026 final in photos: Spain triumphs as football crowns a new champion
items.summary: Spain reclaimed football's biggest prize on Sunday, defeating Argentina 1-0 after extra time in a tense World Cup final.

items.title: China's car market heads for worst year since 2021 as sales plunge 20%
items.summary: Consumer demand for cars in China tumbles following record-high sales of 23.7 million units in 2025.

items.title: From 'Super El Niño' to Europe’s extreme heatwave: How this year's weather shocks are rattling commodity markets
items.summary: Markets are underestimating the impact of climate volatility on a range of asset classes, commodity strategists say.

items.title: Universal and Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' sails to $124.5 million opening
items.summary: Christopher Nolan's "The Odyssey" tallied an estimated $124.5 million at the domestic box office, with IMAX screenings fueling a big haul.

items.title: FBI will no longer investigate ICE confrontations, The New York Times reports
items.summary: The Department of Justice and the Department of Homeland Security have both denied that any change in oversight has taken place.

items.title: 'Give me my money back': South Korean traders' leveraged SK Hynix, Samsung bets unravel after selloff
items.summary: South Korean retail investors suffer heavy losses after Samsung and SK Hynix shares reverse sharply.

items.title: U.S. Treasury yields edge higher as Wall Street monitors Middle East tensions
items.summary: The yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury note — the key benchmark for U.S. government borrowing — rose over 1 basis point to 4.558%.

items.title: Kalshi adds 3 million new users as prediction market capitalizes on the World Cup
items.summary: The prediction market has launched some ads featuring soccer players, as well as formed a partnership to display its brand in more places during the tournament.

items.title: Russia pounds Kyiv as NATO military chief warns Moscow would 'lose a lot' by attacking Baltics
items.summary: Russia and Ukraine traded their heaviest strikes in months, fueling fears of escalation and further strain on oil supplies.

items.title: American Airlines CEO lays out his vision to close a more than $3 billion profit gap
items.summary: The carrier is working on improving reliability, investing in more premium seats and lounges, and considering Boeing and Airbus for a new wide-body plane order.

items.title: Two unexpected revelations in Warren Buffett's CNBC interview
items.summary: Warren Buffett tells CNBC that Bill Gates' ties to Jeffrey Epstein didn't drive his termination of donations.

items.title: Samsung Biologics makes $1.8 billion all-cash offer for Switzerland's PolyPeptide Group
items.summary: South Korea's Samsung Biologics said on Monday that it had made an all-cash bid of 1.46 billion Swiss francs ($1.8 billion) for Switzerland's PolyPeptide Group.

items.title: Korean beauty products are becoming mainstream in the U.S. Why there may be even more growth ahead
items.summary: Morgan Stanley forecasts that K-beauty sales in the U.S. can reach approximately $4 billion in 2026.

items.title: 'WarshGPT': How Wall Street is adapting to the Fed's new era of communication
items.summary: Investment firms are readying for an era with less public commentary from the U.S. central bank. Some are relying on AI for an edge.

items.title: Why Dana White thinks much more of UFC's future will be Power Slap and boxing
items.summary: UFC president and CEO Dana White predicted that Power Slap may become as big as his flagship fighting sport at the Game Plan conference in New York.

items.title: China's Zhongji Innolight sees shares surge after Hong Kong listing approval
items.summary: The deal size will exceed Luxshare Precision's $3.1 billion IPO earlier this month, making it the largest listing in Hong Kong this ​year.

items.title: Plenty of stocks are working in this market — you just have to look beyond tech
items.summary: The big unanswered question is how long there can be such terrific opportunities outside tech before it dawns on those overweight the sector.

items.title: Cybersecurity risks posed by over-the-air tech in autos has analysts concerned
items.summary: The automotive industry's increasing use of over-the-air technology makes it more susceptible to cyberattacks, analysts say.

items.title: Housing costs is the top political issue for young voters, CNBC survey finds
items.summary: The cost of housing is the top priority for voters ages 18 to 34, besting food and healthcare cost concerns, the CNBC All-America Economic Survey found.

items.title: Top Wall Street analysts suggest these 3 dividend stocks for steady income
items.summary: Here are three dividend-paying stocks that are highlighted by Wall Street's top pros, as tracked by TipRanks.

items.title: The market for psychedelic drugs to treat mental health conditions gets a big buy signal
items.summary: Eli Lilly's acquisition of psychedelic drug maker AtaiBeckley extends Big Pharma's embrace of a stigmatized class of medications for treating mental health.
